title: crepis × druceana
intro: Crepis × druceana is a hybrid plant species, derived from the cross between Crepis biennis and Crepis nana, commonly known as the hawk's beard or rosinweed. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Crepis × druceana include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Crepis × druceana is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
